meta.descriptionTemplate: Sell coconut shisha charcoal under your own brand: OEM print across plastic, inner box, master box. Per-design MOQ, single container OK, NDA, proof + sample.

heroSubtitle: White-label production — also called private-label or OEM — lets you sell our coconut shisha charcoal under your own brand. You supply the artwork; we provide the die-line and print it across every layer of the packaging.
upLink: Part of the shisha charcoal packaging system

service.name: White-label coconut shisha charcoal manufacturing
service.description: Private-label (OEM) manufacturing of coconut shisha charcoal: the buyer supplies brand artwork and we print it across the inner plastic, inner box, and master carton, with a die-line provided, an NDA available, and exclusive artwork. Per-design custom-print MOQ; a single branded container is accepted.

hero.ctaQuoteAria: Start a white-label inquiry on WhatsApp

atAGlance.heading: White-label at a glance
atAGlance.singleContainerValue: A single branded container is fine
atAGlance.proofValueTemplate: Digital proof in {{proofTimeDays}} days + physical sample

meaning.p1: White-label means we manufacture the charcoal and you sell it under your brand. The buyer supplies the artwork — we print your design, we don't design it — and we provide the die-line and template so your artwork drops onto the right cutting and fold lines.
meaning.p2: That is the difference from neutral packaging: neutral is unbranded plain stock, white-label is your brand printed across the layers.

photos.alt: Blank versus branded coconut charcoal packaging, before and after
photos.caption: Neutral plain stock vs your brand printed across the set.

surfaces.p1: Your brand can run across every layer: the inner box (your primary branding surface), the master box, the inner plastic, and branded consumables like stickers, tape, and silica sachets.

photos.alt: Complete branded coconut charcoal set: plastic, inner box, master carton together
photos.caption: One brand, printed consistently across all layers.

production.p1: Branded packaging is printed to commercial standards. Print method, press capability, and paper stock are specified per order; FSC-certified paper is available on request.

photos.alt: Offset printing press running coconut charcoal packaging
photos.caption: Commercial offset printing for consistent color.

photos.alt: FSC certification mark on coconut charcoal packaging board
photos.caption: FSC-certified board available on request.

finishes.p1: Finishes form a ladder: a base coating (matte or gloss) comes standard, lamination (matte or gloss film) is an upgrade, and emboss, UV spot, and foil are add-ons layered on top. Each is priced separately.

photos.alt: Finish macro on coconut charcoal packaging: lamination, emboss, UV spot, foil
photos.caption: Lamination, emboss, UV spot, and foil add-ons.

artwork.p1: We print buyer-supplied artwork to print-ready standards. Supply vector files in {{acceptedArtworkFormats}}, set up in {{colorMode}}; we provide the die-line and template for placement.
artwork.pantoneLine: Pantone/spot color matching is available for brand-color accuracy across reorders.

photos.alt: Die-line template render for a coconut charcoal inner box
photos.caption: We supply the die-line; you place your artwork on it.

photos.alt: Pantone color proof sheet for coconut charcoal packaging
photos.caption: Pantone matching keeps brand color consistent across runs.

exclusivity.p1: Your artwork and design are exclusive to you: we never reuse or sell your design to another buyer, and we sign an NDA on request. Printing files and plates are kept on record so reorders match the first run exactly.
exclusivity.p2: Product or territorial exclusivity — restricting the same charcoal to competitors in your market — is a separate arrangement, available by negotiation, not automatic. Artwork exclusivity is the default; territory is a negotiated term.

moq.p1: The custom-print minimum is framed per printed design, distinct from the general order MOQ of {{moqLabel}}. A single branded container is fine — there is no recurring-order commitment — and you can run multiple designs or sizes within one container.

proofSample.p1: Before any production run, your packaging is approved twice: artwork → a digital proof → a physical pre-production sample → your approval → production. Nothing is mass-printed until you sign off on the physical sample.
proofSample.sampleCreditedLine: The sample cost is credited to your first order.

photos.alt: Physical pre-production sample of branded coconut charcoal packaging being reviewed
photos.caption: A physical sample is approved before the production run.

timeline.p1: A first branded order and a reorder run on different clocks.
timeline.firstOrderTemplate: artwork → digital proof ({{proofTimeDays}} days) → physical sample → approval → print → production → ship, about {{newBrandLeadTime}} days from approved artwork.
timeline.reorderTemplate: artwork on file, plates kept — skip design and proof, about {{repeatBrandLeadTime}} days.
timeline.midCtaTitle: Ready to put your brand on the box?
timeline.midCtaText: Send your artwork or ask for the die-line — we'll return a digital proof and a quote.

retail.p1: Retail packaging carries your barcode/EAN (buyer-supplied, factory-printed), country of origin, net weight, and batch/lot code; shelf-ready formats are available.
retail.p2: You own your market's labeling and regulatory compliance — EU CLP, US requirements, health and usage warnings — and supply that artwork; we print it and advise on the UN 1361 dangerous-goods transport markings, which go on the master box.
retail.masterBoxLinkLabel: DG markings on the master box

photos.alt: Retail-ready branded coconut charcoal inner box with barcode and origin markings
photos.caption: Buyer-supplied EAN, origin, and net weight, factory-printed.

whyPrivateLabel.p1: A private label turns a commodity into your brand. Instead of reselling anonymous charcoal, you build brand equity, earn repeat customers who ask for your name, differentiate on packaging and positioning, and control how the product is presented in your market.
whyPrivateLabel.p2: These are durable, qualitative advantages — brand recognition compounds with every container — and they are the reason most serious importers move from neutral to branded once volume is established.

devilsAdvocate.tocLabel: A view from the other side
devilsAdvocate.h2: Should you just stay with neutral packaging instead?
paragraphs: The honest counter-argument: branding is not free. A private label adds a per-design custom-print minimum on top of the order MOQ, print and finish add-ons on top of the charcoal FOB price, and extra lead time for proofing and sampling — about {{newBrandLeadTime}} days on a first order versus shipping neutral stock you can reorder immediately. For a trader who competes purely on landed price, or who is still testing whether the charcoal sells in their market, neutral packaging is the rational choice, and that view genuinely holds at low volume and on the very first shipment.

It stops holding once you intend to reorder. The custom-print minimum is framed per printed design — not per container — and a single branded container is fine, with no recurring commitment, so the entry cost is one run, not a standing obligation. Your artwork is exclusive to you and held on file with the plates, so reorders skip design and proofing and ship in about {{repeatBrandLeadTime}} days rather than {{newBrandLeadTime}}. The added cost is the printing add-on, quoted on top of the same charcoal price you would pay neutral — you are not buying a different product, only your name on it. Neutral wins the first test order; branded wins every container after the brand starts pulling repeat demand.

howToStart.h2: How to start your own brand
howToStart.intro: Seven steps from first contact to a branded container:

steps.label: Inquiry & samples
steps.text: Contact us and request product samples to verify the charcoal.

steps.label: Choose products & packaging
steps.text: Pick the grades, shapes, sizes, and packaging configuration.

steps.label: Submit artwork
steps.text: Send print-ready artwork; we provide the die-line and template.

steps.label: Digital proof
steps.text: We return a digital proof within {{proofTimeDays}} working days.

steps.label: Physical sample
steps.text: We produce a physical pre-production sample for your approval.

steps.label: Approve & produce
steps.text: On approval, we print and produce your branded order.

steps.label: Ship
steps.text: We ship {{portLabel}} with full export documents.

brands.p1: We manufacture for established coconut-charcoal brands across our priority markets. Specific brand examples are shared privately, with permission, or anonymized — we never display a buyer's brand without written consent.

photos.alt: Anonymized examples of branded coconut charcoal packaging
photos.caption: Anonymized brand examples — shown with permission only.

photos.alt: Branded consumables applied to coconut charcoal packaging
photos.caption: Branded consumables complete the private-label set.

prices.intro: Custom-print prices are add-ons on top of the charcoal FOB price — they are not the total. The charcoal price is quoted per SKU on inquiry.

faq.heading: White-label FAQ

items.q: What is white-label / private-label / OEM production?
items.a: White-label (also called private label or OEM) means we manufacture the coconut charcoal and you sell it under your own brand. You supply print-ready artwork; we provide the die-line, print your brand across the packaging layers, and ship the finished SKU under your name.

items.q: What is the minimum to start my own brand?
items.a: The custom-print minimum is framed per printed design, on top of the general order MOQ of {{moqLabel}}. A single branded container is fine — no recurring commitment — and multiple designs or sizes can share one container. The exact per-design minimum is confirmed on inquiry.

items.q: Do I keep my design exclusive, and do you sign an NDA?
items.a: Yes — your artwork and design are exclusive to you, never reused or sold to another buyer, and we sign an NDA on request. Plates and files are kept for reorder consistency. Product or territorial exclusivity can be arranged separately, by negotiation; it is not automatic.

Every layer: the inner box (your primary branding surface), the master box, the inner plastic, and branded consumables — stickers, tape, and silica sachets. You can brand all of them or just the layers that matter for your channel.

items.q: What print methods and finishes do you offer?
items.a: Commercial printing with FSC paper available. Finishes form a distinct ladder: a base matte or gloss coating comes standard, lamination is an upgrade, and emboss, UV spot, and foil are add-ons. Print method and press capability are specified per order.

items.q: What artwork files do you need?
items.a: Print-ready vector files in {{acceptedArtworkFormats}}, set up in {{colorMode}}. We provide the die-line and template for placement, and we match Pantone/spot colors for brand-color accuracy across reorders.

items.q: Do you send a digital proof and physical sample, and is the sample credited?
items.a: Yes to both. You receive a digital proof within {{proofTimeDays}} working days, then a physical pre-production sample before the run. Sample cost and whether it is credited to your order are confirmed on inquiry. Nothing is mass-printed until you approve the physical sample.

items.q: How long does the first branded order take, and are reorders faster?
items.a: A first branded order runs about {{newBrandLeadTime}} days from approved artwork (proof → sample → approval → print → production → ship). Reorders are faster — about {{repeatBrandLeadTime}} days — because artwork and plates are on file, so design and proofing are skipped.

items.q: Who is responsible for my market's labeling and regulatory compliance?
items.a: You do. The buyer owns and supplies the market labeling and regulatory artwork (e.g. EU CLP, US requirements, health and usage warnings). We print your supplied artwork and advise on the UN 1361 dangerous-goods transport markings, which go on the master box.

Seven steps: inquiry and samples, choose products and packaging, submit artwork (we provide the die-line), digital proof, physical sample, approve and produce, then ship {{portLabel}}. Start with an inquiry or a WhatsApp message and we'll send the die-line and a quote.

faq.moreLine: Container counts and general custom-print cost questions are answered on the packaging hub FAQ; order MOQ and payment terms on the general FAQ.
